prompt: Our compliance documentation is perfect for auditors but terrifies new employees. Give it the same content in a welcoming onboarding tone.
I wrote a dense Jupyter notebook with analysis and findings. Repackage the narrative for a slide deck — keep the insights, lose the code blocks.
This Terraform module's docs are written like a reference manual. Convert them to a tutorial format that walks someone through their first deployment step by step.
We need our product FAQ rewritten from a snarky developer voice to a warm customer-support voice. Every answer stays the same, just the personality changes.
Take this 3000-word technical specification and present the same information as a one-page executive brief with bullet points. Nothing gets cut, it just gets compressed and re-presented.
Our error messages are written by engineers for engineers. Rewrite them for end users — same meaning, human-friendly language, no jargon.

This grant proposal is too technical for the non-specialist reviewers on the panel. Adapt it so a scientifically literate but non-expert audience can evaluate the merit without losing any of the methodology details.
I have meeting notes in stream-of-consciousness format. Reshape them into formal minutes with action items, decisions, and attendees — same content, professional structure.
